questions.question: Who scored the famous 'Hand of God' goal in the 1986 World Cup?
questions.image_prompt: Diego Maradona in Argentina blue and white striped jersey, celebrating a goal, 1986 World Cup Mexico, dramatic stadium lighting, oil painting style
questions.explanation: Maradona punched the ball into the net with his hand against England in the 1986 quarterfinal, then scored the 'Goal of the Century' in the same game.
option_explanations: вЭМ Pel√© retired in 1977 вАФ his last World Cup was 1970 in Mexico.
вЬЕ Correct! Maradona's 'Hand of God' is the most controversial goal in World Cup history.
вЭМ Ronaldo (R9) was only 9 years old in 1986. His debut was the 1994 World Cup.
вЭМ Zidane was 14 in 1986. His iconic moments came in 1998 and 2006.

questions.question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?
questions.image_prompt: Brazilian national soccer team celebrating World Cup victory, yellow jerseys, green shorts, confetti falling, trophy held high, vibrant illustration style
questions.explanation: Brazil leads with 5 World Cup titles (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002) and is the only nation to have played in every single World Cup.
option_explanations: вЭМ Germany has 4 titles (1954, 1974, 1990, 2014) вАФ impressive but not the most.
вЭМ Argentina has 3 titles (1978, 1986, 2022) вАФ including Messi's 2022 triumph.
вЬЕ Correct! Brazil's 5 titles is the all-time record. They've never missed a World Cup.
вЭМ Italy has 4 titles (1934, 1938, 1982, 2006) вАФ tied with Germany.

questions.question: Which player holds the record for most Premier League goals of all time?
questions.image_prompt: English Premier League striker celebrating a goal in a packed stadium, black and white striped jersey, crowd roaring, dynamic sports art style
questions.explanation: Alan Shearer scored 260 Premier League goals for Blackburn Rovers and Newcastle United between 1992вАУ2006. The record still stands.
option_explanations: вЭМ Wayne Rooney scored 208 PL goals вАФ great but 52 short of Shearer.
вЭМ Frank Lampard scored 177 PL goals вАФ incredible for a midfielder.
вЭМ Andrew Cole scored 187 PL goals вАФ deadly but behind Shearer.
вЬЕ Correct! Shearer's 260 goals is the untouchable all-time PL record.

questions.question: How many Ballon d'Or awards has Lionel Messi won?
questions.image_prompt: Soccer legend in Argentina blue and white jersey lifting a golden trophy award, stadium lights, confetti, painterly dramatic style
questions.explanation: Messi has won 8 Ballon d'Or awards вАФ the most in history. His 8th came in 2023 following Argentina's 2022 World Cup triumph.
option_explanations: вЭМ 6 was his count before 2021. He has since won two more.
вЭМ 7 was correct after 2021 вАФ but he won his 8th in 2023.
вЬЕ Correct! Messi's 8 Ballon d'Or awards is the all-time record as of 2023.
вЭМ 9 is not correct вАФ Messi has 8. Ronaldo has 5 by comparison.

questions.question: Which club has won the most UEFA Champions League titles?
questions.image_prompt: Real Madrid players in white jerseys celebrating Champions League victory, giant trophy, Santiago Bernabeu stadium, golden confetti, epic illustration
questions.explanation: Real Madrid has won the Champions League/European Cup a record 15 times, including 5 times in just 9 years between 2014 and 2022.
option_explanations: вЭМ Barcelona has 5 Champions League titles вАФ impressive but far behind Real Madrid.
вЬЕ Correct! Real Madrid's 15 UCL titles is the all-time record by a wide margin.
вЭМ Bayern Munich has 6 Champions League titles вАФ third most in history.
вЭМ AC Milan has 7 Champions League titles вАФ second most, but still behind Real Madrid.

questions.question: Which club has won the most Premier League titles?
questions.image_prompt: Manchester United players in red jerseys lifting the Premier League trophy, Old Trafford stadium packed with fans, confetti, triumphant digital art
questions.explanation: Manchester United has won 20 Premier League/First Division titles, 13 of which came in the Premier League era under Sir Alex Ferguson.
option_explanations: вЭМ Liverpool has 19 league titles вАФ one behind United, with their last PL title in 2020.
вЭМ Arsenal has 13 league titles, including 3 PL titles and the famous 2003вАУ04 Invincibles season.
вЭМ Chelsea has 6 Premier League titles вАФ very successful in the modern era but far behind United.
вЬЕ Correct! Manchester United's 20 titles is the most in English football history.

questions.question: Pel√© scored how many official career goals in total?
questions.image_prompt: Pel√© in Brazil yellow jersey, dribbling past defenders, 1970 World Cup era, vintage retro poster art style, warm golden tones
questions.explanation: Pel√© scored 757 officially recognized goals in his career according to RSSSF. He is widely considered the greatest footballer of all time, winning 3 World Cups with Brazil.
option_explanations: вЭМ 643 is too low вАФ Pel√© was far more prolific than that across his full career.
вЬЕ Correct! Pel√© scored 757 officially recognized career goals вАФ a staggering achievement.
вЭМ 1279 is a figure sometimes cited but includes unofficial friendlies not recognized by FIFA.
вЭМ 541 is way too low вАФ that figure doesn't represent Pel√©'s incredible output.

questions.question: Who scored the winning goal in the 1999 Champions League final for Manchester United?
questions.image_prompt: Manchester United player in red jersey scoring a last-minute goal, Camp Nou Barcelona stadium, wild celebration, dramatic night game lighting, digital painting
questions.explanation: Ole Gunnar Solskjaer scored in the 93rd minute to complete Manchester United's dramatic 2вАУ1 comeback against Bayern Munich at Camp Nou, completing the treble.
option_explanations: вЭМ Andy Cole was a key striker that season but did not score the winner in the final.
вЭМ Dwight Yorke scored crucial goals that season but not the famous 93rd minute winner.
вЭМ Teddy Sheringham scored the equalizer in the 91st minute вАФ vital, but not the winner.
вЬЕ Correct! Solskjaer poked home in the 93rd minute to complete one of football's greatest comebacks.

questions.question: Which country did Zinedine Zidane represent internationally?
questions.image_prompt: Zinedine Zidane in French blue national team jersey, mid dribble, elegant and powerful, Stade de France, artistic portrait style
questions.explanation: Zidane played for France, winning the 1998 World Cup and 2000 European Championship. He was born in Marseille to Algerian immigrant parents.
option_explanations: вЭМ Algeria is Zidane's family's country of origin вАФ his parents are Algerian вАФ but he was born in France and chose to represent Les Bleus.
вЭМ Morocco is not correct вАФ Zidane has no connection to Morocco.
вЬЕ Correct! Zidane represented France, scoring twice in the 1998 World Cup final vs Brazil.
вЭМ Spain is not correct вАФ Zidane managed Real Madrid but always played for France.

questions.question: Which manager won the most Premier League titles?

questions.explanation: Sir Alex Ferguson won 13 Premier League titles with Manchester United between 1993 and 2013 вАФ more than any other manager in history.
option_explanations: вЭМ Pep Guardiola has won 6 Premier League titles with Man City вАФ a remarkable record but behind Ferguson.

вЬЕ Correct! Sir Alex Ferguson's 13 PL titles with Manchester United is the untouchable record.
